metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in S2 how `assemble_merge_change_context`'s output reaches `CodeReviewBatchRunner.context` without being re-wrapped by `compose_diff_context` — either add a `context_override: str | None = None` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict` (bypassing `assemble_diff_context` when supplied), or specify that the merge adapter path constructs `CodeReviewBatchRunner` directly with the merge context string, bypassing `adapter.code_review_decision`'s current `produce_code_review_verdict` call signature.
findings.finding: The data-flow path from `assemble_merge_change_context` to the `CodeReviewBatchRunner.context` is unspecified. The plan adds `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff, mergelist)` in `assemble.py` producing a `## Merge context / ## Auto-merge diff` string, but `produce_code_review_verdict` (called by the existing `adapter.code_review_decision`) always calls `assemble.assemble_diff_context(diff_text=...)` which passes `dc.context` — the standard `## Changed files / ## Orientation / ## Diff` wrapper — to `CodeReviewBatchRunner`. If the merge path passes the merge-context string as `diff_text` to `adapter.code_review_decision`, it will be double-wrapped in `compose_diff_context`'s `## Diff` block. The plan does not specify whether (a) `produce_code_review_verdict` gains a `context_override` kwarg to bypass `assemble_diff_context`, (b) the merge path bypasses `adapter.code_review_decision` entirely and constructs `CodeReviewBatchRunner` directly with the merge context, or (c) the re-wrapping is accepted as-is. Without this specification, the merge context assembled in S2 will not reach the batch runner in the documented `## Merge context` format.
findings.location: S2 — 'Merge-context contract: a new `assemble_merge_change_context` co-located with `DiffContext` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`'; src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py:169–175; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:436–458
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `context_override: str | None = None` kwarg to `produce_code_review_verdict`; when set, skip `assemble_diff_context` and use `context_override` directly as `CodeReviewBatchRunner(context=context_override)`. The merge path in voter/adapter passes the `assemble_merge_change_context` output as `context_override`. This is a minimal, targeted change that preserves the existing non-merge path entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2 / gate_dispatch.py + voter.py**: Specify the exact call-site wiring — either (a) add a `context_override: str | None` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict` so the voter can pass the assembled merge context directly, or (b) define a `MergeDiffContext` that satisfies the same interface as `DiffContext` and is passed where `dc` is today — and record this in the S2 edit targets.
findings.finding: The plan never specifies how the `assemble_merge_change_context` output string flows into the existing `produce_code_review_verdict` / `CodeReviewBatchRunner` gate machinery. The current dispatch calls `assemble.assemble_diff_context(...)` → `DiffContext` → `CodeReviewBatchRunner(context=dc.context)`. The plan adds a new assembler function but defines no new parameter on `produce_code_review_verdict`, no new context class, and no alternate dispatch path — leaving the wiring from merge context string to gate invocation unspecified.
findings.location: S2 — 'Merge-context contract: a new `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff, mergelist)` co-located with `DiffContext` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `monitoring_merge_change.tf` (or extend an existing monitoring file) with a CloudWatch alarm on the `merge_change_409_guard` metric (threshold > 0, treat_missing_data = notBreaching, wired to `aws_sns_topic.alerts`), matching the voter_errors alarm pattern in `monitoring_s4b.tf`, and reference it explicitly in the S2 observability bullet.
findings.finding: The plan commits to new structured metric signals for the merge-change path (merge_change_review, merge_change_409_guard, mergelist_fetch_error via observability.sh) but specifies no corresponding CloudWatch alarm Terraform resource, unlike the established precedent for voter_errors (monitoring_s4b.tf) and replication_errors (monitoring_s5.tf). The merge_change_409_guard signal is operationally critical: it fires when the bot hits the GetPatch 409 path (fail-closed -1 on every merge change), which would silently break the entire feature-branch pattern. Without a CloudWatch alarm on this metric, a regression to the 409 path goes undetected until an operator notices stalled merge changes.
findings.location: S2 — Observability bullet ('a journald-pattern metric wired via infra/scripts/observability.sh (modelled on voter_errors)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `infra/terraform/monitoring_merge_change.tf` with an `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` for `merge_change_409_guard` (namespace `rebar/host`, statistic `Sum`, threshold `0`, `comparison_operator = "GreaterThanThreshold"`, `treat_missing_data = "notBreaching"`, `alarm_actions = [aws_sns_topic.alerts.arn]`). Update S2's AC to reference this Terraform resource explicitly alongside the observability.sh wiring, mirroring the S4b/S5 precedent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S2, specify the call-contract change between `voter.py` and `adapter.py` for merge changes — either a new `is_merge: bool` / `merge_context` parameter on `code_review_decision`, or a separate `merge_change_decision(...)` function in `adapter.py`, so the new `_TAG_SUFFIXES` key is actually reachable.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'extend `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` with a merge-change variant' and a new robot comment format `[LLM-Review: PASS (merge-change, N integrated commits)]`, but does not specify how `code_review_decision` in `adapter.py` learns that it is processing a merge change. The current call signature `code_review_decision(diff_text, repo_root, ref, *, config)` carries no merge-context flag. Without a new parameter (e.g. `is_merge_change: bool` or a merge-specific context object), the voter cannot cause `adapter.py` to select the new tag suffix — making the robot comment format change a dead letter.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path / adapter.py `_TAG_SUFFIXES` and robot comment

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pre-decide the g2p dispatch config for feature/* branches: either confirm in the plan (via a pre-reading of g2p source) that no config is needed and document why, OR specify the exact `gerrit_to_platform.ini` key/value required for feature/* dispatch so the executor authors it deterministically rather than discovering it mid-task.
findings.finding: S3 defers a real configuration decision to the executor via a 'determine from g2p source/docs whether' investigation: if gerrit_to_platform.ini does filter by branch, new config must be written; if not, nothing changes. The plan does not pre-decide which path to take or provide a default config value, leaving the executor to discover and author the required config during execution.
findings.location: S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes (first sentence of AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in S3 like: 'Pre-checked: g2p dispatches all refs matching patchset-created regardless of branch (source: <file>:<line>); no ini change required — verified in S5 run logs.' OR specify: 'Add `branches = refs/heads/feature/*` under the [dispatch] section of gerrit_to_platform.ini if the current config has a branch allowlist.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict (line 57) to include `["feature-branch-drivers"]=1` so the group's UUID is fetched from the live group list and written into the `groups` file alongside Administrators and Service Users.
findings.finding: setup-project.sh hardcodes only `Administrators` and `Service Users` in its `want` groups dict (line 57: `declare -A want=( ["Administrators"]=1 ["Service Users"]=1 )`), which controls which group UUIDs are written into the `groups` file when pushing refs/meta/config. The plan specifies adding a new `feature-branch-drivers` group to project.config ACLs (S1), but does NOT mention updating `setup-project.sh`'s `want` dict to include that group. Gerrit validates refs/meta/config and will reject the push if a referenced group's UUID is absent from the `groups` file.
findings.location: S1 — Gate + ACL config / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 57
findings.suggested_fix: In setup-project.sh line 57, change to: `declare -A want=( ["Administrators"]=1 ["Service Users"]=1 ["feature-branch-drivers"]=1 )`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `feature-branch-drivers` to the `want` array in `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` (line 57) so its UUID is fetched and included in the `groups` file when the new ACLs are pushed.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` is not named as an S1 edit target, but it must be updated when `feature-branch-drivers` is added to `project.config`: lines 57–63 of `setup-project.sh` hard-code `declare -A want=(["Administrators"]=1 ["Service Users"]=1)` and look up UUIDs only for those two groups. Adding `feature-branch-drivers` to `project.config` without adding it to `want` will cause `git push HEAD:refs/meta/config` to be rejected by Gerrit (missing group UUID in the `groups` file).
findings.location: S1 — Gate + ACL config / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 57
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 57 of setup-project.sh to: `declare -A want=( ["Administrators"]=1 ["Service Users"]=1 ["feature-branch-drivers"]=1 )`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in S2 that `get_commit` is called on every patchset; either cache the value from the webhook payload's `patchSet.parents` count (avoiding the extra round-trip entirely) or add an explicit timeout + fallback (treat as non-merge, log a warning) and note the per-review latency budget impact.
findings.finding: Every patchset review (not just merge commits) will now make an unconditional extra Gerrit REST call — `revisions/{rev}/commit` for parent-count detection — added to the hot path inside `review_and_vote` before any diff fetch. This new API call is on the critical path for ALL reviews, silently increasing baseline latency and adding a new failure point that can slow or block normal single-parent change reviews if Gerrit is slow or rate-limiting.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path: 'merge detection via revisions/{rev}/commit parent count (>=2) runs INSIDE review_and_vote immediately after the existing-vote check and BEFORE any diff fetch'
findings.suggested_fix: Gerrit webhook patchset-created events include parent SHAs in the payload; use `len(patchset['parents']) >= 2` from the payload instead of a separate REST call. For the reconciler path, the existing change-detail fetch (used for other fields) can be extended to include commit parents, avoiding a dedicated get_commit call.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2 / adapter.py**: Define the exact key string(s) for the new `_TAG_SUFFIXES` entries (e.g. `"merge-pass"` → `"PASS (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)"` and `"merge-block"` → `"BLOCK — finding (merge-change)"`), specify which key is passed as `reason` from the merge-change path in `voter.py`, and assert the vocabulary in the existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES`-assertion test.
findings.finding: The plan mandates extending `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` with a merge-change variant (e.g. `[LLM-Review: PASS (merge-change, N integrated commits)]`), but never defines the KEY string for this new entry. The existing `_message_tag(reason)` does `_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]` — passing an undefined key raises `KeyError` at runtime on any merge-change review path.
findings.location: S2 — Robot comment: 'extend `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` with a merge-change variant'
findings.suggested_fix: Add two new keys to `_TAG_SUFFIXES`: e.g. `"merge-pass": "PASS (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)"` (rendered via `.format(n=n)`) and `"merge-block": "BLOCK — finding (merge-change)"`. Update `_message_tag` to accept an optional `n` parameter for the count substitution. In the voter's merge path, pass `reason="merge-pass"` or `"merge-block"` accordingly.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S2, specify the behavioral contract for `mergelist_fetch_error` (e.g. fail-closed: post a robot comment explaining the fetch failure and cast LLM-Review=0 or -1, do not vote +1) and add a unit test asserting that contract, analogous to the existing 409 fail-closed test.
findings.finding: The behavioral contract for `mergelist_fetch_error` is unspecified — the plan names it as a structured log event but does not state what the bot does when the mergelist fetch fails (fail-closed with a -1 vote, abstain with a comment, or proceed on mergelist-only context). Without a defined contract, the S2 'unit + fixture tests for all of the above' cannot meaningfully assert the correct error-handling behavior, leaving a failure path of the new merge-change review flow untested.
findings.location: S2 — Review-bot merge-change path, 'Observability: structured log events for … mergelist_fetch_error'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the S2 spec: 'On `mergelist_fetch_error`, the bot MUST NOT cast a positive vote; it posts a robot comment (e.g. "[LLM-Review: ERROR — could not fetch merge list; rerun with /rerun]") and returns without voting (or casts -1). A unit test mocks `get_mergelist` raising an exception and asserts (a) no +1 is cast, (b) the error comment is posted, and (c) the `mergelist_fetch_error` log event fires.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **S2 / voter.py merge path**: Specify the fallback for `get_mergelist` failure — either (a) proceed with auto-merge-diff-only context and log `mergelist_fetch_error` as a WARNING (degraded but not blocked), or (b) treat it as a coverage-gap BLOCK (fail-closed); record which posture is chosen and add it to the merge-change path spec.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a `mergelist_fetch_error` structured log event but defines no fallback decision for what `voter.py` does when `get_mergelist` fails — unlike every other error path (review-error, review_setup GerritError, post_vote GerritError) which have explicit, documented fallback behaviors (fail-closed BLOCK with a specific reason).
findings.location: S2 — 'Observability: structured log events for ... `mergelist_fetch_error`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a defined RETIRE path for abandoned/expired feature branches to S6: specify who has authority to delete (feature-branch-drivers), require that all open changes targeting the branch are explicitly abandoned in Gerrit before the ref is deleted, and document this as a step in the branch-lifetime-cap enforcement procedure.
findings.finding: The RETIRE path for feature branches covers only the post-merge-back case (scripted/manual deletion after the merge change lands). There is no defined cleanup path for abandoned feature branches — features that are cancelled, stalled beyond the lifetime cap, or whose merge-back is never executed. These refs will accumulate indefinitely on the Gerrit server.
findings.location: Locked design — Branch namespace; S6 — Process + back-out

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the S4 CONTRIBUTING.md merge-back recipe to include a conflict-resolution sub-path: steps for `git merge --no-ff` with conflicts → resolve markers → `git add` → `git merge --continue` (or `git commit`) → verify the commit message contains a Change-Id (the hook runs on commit, so it should be present) → the amend step is still needed if the hook was not installed before the merge commit → push to refs/for/main.
findings.finding: The S4 merge-back recipe covers the clean/happy-path merge (hook install, --no-ff commit, Change-Id amend, push to refs/for/main) and two error sub-paths (pushMerge refusal, missing Change-Id). It does not cover the conflict-resolution sub-path: what a developer does when `git merge --no-ff feature/<name>` produces conflicts — how to resolve, stage the resolution, complete the merge commit while preserving the Change-Id stamp, and then push. S5 scenario 5 proves the bot handles this case, but the developer recipe for reaching that state is absent from the documentation spec.
findings.location: S4 — Documentation sweep; CONTRIBUTING.md merge-back recipe spec

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S3, explicitly verify and document whether g2p's `comment-added` hook dispatch (the `recheck` path) is branch-agnostic for `feature/*` refs, separate from and in addition to the already-confirmed `patchset-created` webhook branch-agnosticism.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the review-bot webhook is CONFIRMED branch-agnostic (webhooks.config has no branch filter)' and uses this to conclude that 'BOTH votes gate every feature-branch change (R3)'. Reading webhooks.config, the webhook fires only on `event = patchset-created` — there is NO `comment-added` event configured. The plan's S3 also states that CI re-runs on re-merge via 'patchset-created / comment-added `recheck`' (from epic 1fa8's g2p hooks). These are two separate delivery mechanisms (webhook plugin vs. g2p hooks), but the plan blurs them in the Locked design section's branch-agnosticism claim, creating ambiguity about whether the RECHECK path (comment-added → g2p → CI) is also branch-agnostic for feature/* refs.
findings.location: Locked design — Story loop / infra/gerrit/webhooks.config

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In S3, replace the assumed `gerrit-verify.yaml` filename with the actual filename delivered by epic 1fa8 (verify against the merged state of 1fa8 before S3 begins, or add a note that the filename must be confirmed from the 1fa8 merge commit).
findings.finding: `gerrit-verify.yaml` does not exist in `.github/workflows/` — the plan (S3) references it as an existing CI workflow to verify against feature branches and merge changes, but the file is absent from the repo. It is a deliverable of the prerequisite epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug), which the plan states must be completed first. If 1fa8's deliverable name differs from `gerrit-verify.yaml` (prior reviews show debate between `gerrit-verify.yaml` and `gerrit-verify-manual-dispatch.yaml`), S3's done-definition referencing this file will be broken.
findings.location: S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before finalizing the copyCondition amendment in S1, verify that `changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` is a recognized token in the running Gerrit 3.14.1 — either by a targeted REST/SSH config-test probe on the live instance or by confirming the Gerrit 3.14.x release notes show this changekind was introduced at or before 3.14.1; add this verification as an explicit S1 prerequisite step parallel to the S2 fixture-capture discipline.
findings.finding: The plan's LLM-Review copyCondition amendment to `changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` is an unverified capability assertion against the specific running Gerrit 3.14.1. The plan cites config-labels.html but does not require verifying that MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is a recognized changekind token in Gerrit 3.14.1 before committing the design to it. If the token was introduced in a later Gerrit minor version, the config push will either silently ignore the clause (making re-merge votes require a full re-review, defeating the design goal) or reject the config outright. The plan's own rigor on the files/ fixture (S2: 'capture a live fixture… BEFORE building on it') is not applied symmetrically here.
findings.location: Locked design / Decision log — 'LLM-Review copyCondition gains changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE'; S1 AC — 'LLM-Review copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S1 AC: 'Verify MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is recognized by running Gerrit 3.14.1 BEFORE applying the copyCondition change — push a minimal test project.config with only the new clause to a scratch project and confirm Gerrit accepts it without error; record the probe result on the ticket alongside the submit-type prior-state recording.' This mirrors the S2 fixture discipline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the mergelist commit-count cap as a named constant (e.g. `MERGE_COMMIT_LIST_CAP = 100`) in `assemble.py` alongside `DIFF_CHAR_CAP`, and reference it by name in the S2 spec rather than as a bare literal.
findings.finding: The 100-commit truncation cap for the mergelist section is stated as a bare literal in the plan (`capped at 100 with truncation notice`) without being designated a named constant. Unlike DIFF_CHAR_CAP (explicitly called out as a reused named constant), this threshold is expected to evolve as teams learn what context volume is useful for LLM merge reviews, and hardcoding it as a magic number in assemble.py would make it harder to tune.
findings.location: S2 — 'assemble_merge_change_context … integrated-commit subject lines, capped at 100 with truncation notice'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `MERGE_COMMIT_LIST_CAP = 100` to the same constants block as `DIFF_CHAR_CAP` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit bot-rollback step to S6: specify the command/procedure to revert or redeploy the previous bot image/commit, confirm the fail-closed 409 behavior (pre-S2) is safe as a fallback state, and note that ACL revocation is sufficient to prevent new merge changes even while the rollback proceeds.
findings.finding: S6 specifies rollback for Gerrit config changes (ACLs, submit-type pin) but does not explicitly address rolling back the bot code deployment (voter.py, gerrit_client.py, adapter.py, assemble.py). If the new merge-change path misbehaves in production, the operator has no documented quick-revert procedure for the bot service itself alongside the config rollback steps.
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out
findings.suggested_fix: In S6, add: 'Bot rollback: redeploy the previous tagged bot release (or `git revert` the S2 commits and re-deploy); the pre-S2 bot is safe for normal changes (fail-closed -1 on any 409, which cannot occur unless someone pushes a merge change — itself blocked by ACL revocation). Bot rollback is therefore decoupled from and slower-path than ACL revocation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `MAX_MERGE_DIFF_FILES` constant (configurable alongside DIFF_CHAR_CAP) in assemble.py; break/skip further `get_file_diff` calls once the running char total reaches DIFF_CHAR_CAP, and log the number of files fetched vs skipped so operators can see truncation in the structured `merge_change_review` event.
findings.finding: The auto-merge diff assembly loop (`get_file_diff` called per file returned by `get_merge_files`) is an N+1 API call pattern with no stated bound on the number of files in a conflict resolution. The plan caps total content at DIFF_CHAR_CAP but does not limit or batch the underlying API calls, so a merge change touching many files (e.g. a large conflict or a catch-up merge after significant main divergence) makes an unbounded number of sequential Gerrit REST calls.
findings.location: S2 — gerrit_client.py: 'get_merge_files (no parent), get_file_diff (auto-merge base)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify concrete values for the branch-lifetime cap (e.g., maximum calendar days or sprint count) and catch-up cadence (e.g., 'merge main→feature at least every N days or whenever main advances by M commits') so the executor can author the ADR and CONTRIBUTING.md entries deterministically.
findings.finding: The branch-lifetime cap and catch-up cadence are referenced as policy constraints in multiple places (Context, Sequencing & Risk, S6) but no concrete values are given anywhere in the plan. S6 says they are 'documented' as outputs, but the executor must invent the values — a real design choice deferred to the executor.
findings.location: S6 — Process + back-out (first bullet); Context (OSS caveat); Sequencing & Risk (last sentence of Risk section)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to S6: 'Branch-lifetime cap: 30 calendar days (matching OpenDev guidance). Catch-up cadence: merge main into the feature branch at minimum once per week or whenever main advances by 5+ commits, whichever comes first. These values are the defaults; the ADR records them as the baseline policy.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in S2 that the existing gerrit_client retry/backoff mechanism covers all four new methods, OR add per-method retry with backoff and a fail-closed fallback (abstain / -1 with comment) if retries are exhausted on get_merge_files or get_mergelist; include a test for the transient-failure path.
findings.finding: Retry/backoff coverage for the four new `gerrit_client.py` methods (`get_commit`, `get_merge_files`, `get_file_diff`, `get_mergelist`) is not addressed. The plan specifies error observability (`mergelist_fetch_error` log event) but does not state whether the existing client's retry/backoff decorator (if any) is automatically applied to new methods, or whether explicit handling is required for transient failures in these calls.
findings.location: S2 — gerrit_client.py new methods; Reliability section of S2 observability spec

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] S1 ADR: specify the `feature-branch-drivers` group membership management process — who grants/revokes membership (e.g. Gerrit Administrators only, via a tracked ticket), an audit cadence (e.g. reviewed on each feature branch deletion), and whether a group membership change requires a reviewed change to `refs/meta/config` or an admin SSH command (record the control in the ADR alongside the membership policy statement).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `feature-branch-drivers` Gerrit ACL group with `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` on a public-internet–reachable Gerrit instance, but specifies no membership management, audit, or offboarding mechanism — only 'membership policy recorded in the ADR'. An unauthorized or stale group member can push merge commits to `refs/for/refs/heads/main` (though the two-vote gate still blocks actual submit). The group membership path is an unspecified least-privilege surface on an internet-reachable boundary.
findings.location: Locked design — ACL section; S1 AC (`feature-branch-drivers` group); ADR referenced but not defined

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
